title: Subquery produces wrong result when set big_tables = 1
poc: CREATE TABLE t0 AS SELECT * FROM t1;
CREATE TABLE t3(a INT, b INT);
select a from t1 join (select count(a) as cnt from t2 union select 1 limit 1, 1) as derived_1_2(cnt) on (true) where t1.a > derived_1_2.cnt;
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t4(a INT, b VARCHAR(10), c FLOAT, d DATE);
CREATE TABLE t5(e INT, f INT DEFAULT 0, g TEXT NOT NULL);
SELECT a FROM t4 JOIN (SELECT COUNT(e) AS cnt FROM t5 WHERE f IS NULL UNION SELECT 1 LIMIT 0, 1) AS derived_1_3(cnt) ON (true) WHERE t4.a > derived_1_3.cnt;
